The key policy rate is expected to remain unchanged at 5.25%. This pause reflects a cautious approach amid the West Asia conflict. The conflict poses risks to inflation and economic growth. items.title: Global Market: Fed's Daly dismisses AI as near-term inflation risk amid rising prices
items.summary: San Francisco Fed President Mary Daly stated that while Artificial Intelligence may lower prices long-term, it's not a current concern for monetary policy. She believes AI's economic impact will unfold over five to ten years, boosting productivity. Daly attributed current inflation to tariffs and recent energy/food cost hikes, not AI, emphasizing the Fed's focus on the next 12 months.

items.title: After courts, RBI, independent reviewers clear HDFC Bank; persistence of crisis narrative raises questions

The bank remains systemically critical and financially sound.

The world's multipolar shift is seen as beneficial for India.

items.title: BIT sweeter? India weighs easing treaty rules with safeguards to attract foreign capital
items.summary: As the world shifts economically, India is set to revamp its investment treaty frameworks. The government is exploring options to simplify the timelines for foreign entities aiming for global arbitration. This proactive step is aimed at enticing foreign investment into the country, while also incorporating safeguards that will preserve India's sovereign policy-making authority and deter any potential exploitation of treaties.

items.title: FDI equity inflows up 18% in FY26, US investments double
items.summary: India saw a significant jump in foreign investment in the last fiscal year. FDI equity inflows increased by 18 percent, reaching $58.84 billion. The United States and Singapore were major contributors to this growth. Overall FDI, including reinvested earnings, also saw a substantial rise. Key sectors attracting investment included computer software and hardware, services, and trading.

items.title: Coal ministry invites applications under gasification projects

items.title: PM rooftop solar scheme set for 7.5 million milestone

The scheme, launched in February 2024, aims to boost rooftop solar adoption, especially for families using 1-3 kW of electricity. Over 6.5 million applications are in the pipeline, indicating strong public interest and momentum.

items.title: FEMA case against Myntra closed following compounding: ED

items.summary: Global liquidity tailwinds are unlikely to get any better from now and that makes Arvind Sanger, Managing Partner at Geosphere Capital Management “cautiously optimistic” on the Indian markets.

items.title: Automation threatens 69% jobs in India: World Bank
items.summary: "In large parts of Africa, it is likely that technology could fundamentally disrupt this pattern. Research based on World Bank data has predicted that the proportion of jobs threatened in India by automation is 69 percent, in China it is 77 percent and in Ethiopia, the percentage of jobs threatened by automation is 85 percent," he said.
